questions: How is bookkeeping different from accounting?
What tools are used for bookkeeping?
answers: Bookkeeping is the systematic recording of daily financial transactions — sales, purchases, receipts, and payments — into organized accounts.
Bookkeeping holo doinondin financial transaction gulo — bikri, kena, receipt, payment — niyomito bhabe record kora.
Bookkeeping is the foundation of accounting — it records transactions, while accounting analyzes, interprets, and reports on that recorded data for decision-making.
Common bookkeeping tools include QuickBooks, Xero, FreshBooks, Wave, and even Excel spreadsheets. These help automate recording and organizing financial data.

feelings.question: bookkeeping ki?
feelings.answer: Yes, Bookkeeping is the systematic recording of daily financial transactions into organized accounts.
